column13: Description
Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each order transaction row; used as the join anchor in analytical queries against this fact table
Foreign key linking to dim_date; enables time-based GROUP BY and WHERE filters across day, month, quarter, and year without storing redundant date attributes in the fact table
Foreign key linking to dim_customer; supports JOIN to customer demographics and segment data; a missing index on this column can turn a two-second query into a two-minute bottleneck
Foreign key linking to dim_product; central to star schema spoke joins; in a snowflake schema this key may instead reference dim_product_subcategory
Foreign key linking to dim_store; enables regional and geographic aggregations; part of the spoke pattern in the star schema surrounding this fact table
Additive measure recording the number of units sold in a single order line; safe to SUM across all dimension grain levels without distortion
Price per unit at time of sale; stored in the fact table rather than dim_product to preserve historical accuracy and prevent update anomalies when product prices change
Monetary discount applied to the order line; defaults to 0.00 when no discount is given; NULLs are not permitted to avoid aggregation errors in SUM calculations
Calculated as (quantity_sold * unit_price) - discount_amount; stored as a derived column for query performance; formula: =quantity_sold * unit_price - discount_amount
Flag indicating whether the order line was subsequently returned; used in WHERE clauses to filter analytical reports to completed sales only
System timestamp recording when the row was inserted into the database; used for auditing and incremental ETL loads; not intended for business-level date filtering
Description
Surrogate primary key for the customer dimension; referenced by fact_orders.customer_key; using a surrogate key instead of the source system ID protects the schema from upstream data changes

Natural key from the source transactional system; indexed with a UNIQUE constraint to prevent duplicate customer records entering the dimension; useful for tracing rows back to the operational database
Customer first name; stored separately from last_name in compliance with 1NF atomicity rules — combining names into one column would violate first normal form and complicate WHERE filtering
Customer last name; separated from first_name to satisfy 1NF; enables ORDER BY last_name and precise WHERE last_name = filtering in analytical queries
Customer email; indexed to support fast lookup queries; NULLs permitted for legacy records migrated before email capture was mandatory
Business-defined segment label such as Premium, Standard, or Wholesale; used heavily in GROUP BY clauses for segmentation reports; defaults to Standard when no segment is assigned
Geographic region of the customer; stored in dim_customer rather than a separate dim_region table in a star schema design; a snowflake schema would normalise this into its own table

Country of the customer; functionally dependent only on customer_key and not on any non-key column, satisfying 3NF; removing transitive dependencies between region and country was a key normalisation step
Date the customer first registered; used in date arithmetic queries such as calculating customer tenure; JOIN to dim_date via this column requires a date-to-key conversion
Indicates whether the customer account is currently active; filter with WHERE is_active = TRUE in operational reports to exclude churned customers from revenue summaries
Description
Surrogate primary key for the product dimension; referenced by fact_orders.product_key; insulates the schema from changes to source product codes, preventing update anomalies in the fact table
